attestation.statement: Clinician confirmed in writing having clinically reviewed all 51 answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation)

transform_flags: Source filename is 'Cardiac Arrest AUC-021.txt' but the note's own internal 'Case ID' field reads AUC-022 — a source-data discrepancy, flagged per protocol rather than silently resolved; case_id SEQ (00022) was derived from the internal Case ID field as the more specific identifier. This also means the source folder has two distinct files both externally labelled AUC-021 (Burns and this Cardiac Arrest note) — maintainer should reconcile source filenames.

This is a bystander/telephone-triage-style case; almost all history came via a third party (adult child) rather than the unresponsive patient — disclosure_gate values were set accordingly within the fixed schema enum (volunteered_unprompted used for bystander-volunteered facts, as no dedicated third-party gate exists in the schema).

Source note describes in-person CPR, AED shock delivery, and clinician presence already having occurred; presentation layer models the case at the point of initial telehealth/telephone contact per protocol reprojection rules — i.e., before any clinician physically arrived.
Exact age not stated in source; a plausible age was assigned as a conservative placeholder.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
